dublinCore.dc:title: 119 HR 8481 RH: Kayleigh’s Law Act of 2026
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

action.action-desc: Mr. Hamadeh of Arizona (for himself, Mr. Biggs of Arizona, Mr. Burchett, Mr. Schweikert, Mr. Joyce of Ohio, Mr. Downing, Mr. Babin, Mr. Taylor, Ms. King-Hinds, Mr. Palmer, Ms. Mace, Mr. Stutzman, Mr. Crane, Mr. Gosar, Mr. Moore of Alabama, Mr. Ciscomani, Mr. Meuser, and Mr. Steube) introduced the following bill; which was referred to the Committee on the Judiciary
Additional sponsors: Mr. Smith of New Jersey, Mr. Ezell, Mr. Gill of Texas, and Mr. Calvert
Reported with an amendment, committed to the Committee of the Whole House on the State of the Union, and ordered to be printed
action.action-instruction: Strike out all after the enacting clause and insert the part printed in italic
For text of introduced bill, see copy of bill as introduced on April 23, 2026

form.official-title: To amend title 18, United States Code, to provide for the issuance of natural lifetime injunctions for certain victims.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the Kayleigh’s Law Act of 2026.

subsection.text: Chapter 238 of title 18, United States Code, is amended by adding at the end the following:

In the case of any defendant convicted of a covered offense, the court shall, on motion of the Government (with the consent of a victim) or a victim, issue, at the time of sentencing, an order prohibiting contact with a victim. Such order shall remain in effect for the life of the defendant, subject to subsection (b). The imposition of such order shall be included in and served on the defendant during any sentencing proceeding. A violation of such order shall be punishable as a contempt of court.

paragraph.text: An order issued under subsection (a) may be terminated or suspended only—

subparagraph.text: on motion of the victim to whom such order pertains, alleging—

clause.text: that the conviction was the subject of a pardon or commutation; or

subparagraph.text: on motion of the defendant, alleging that the conviction was dismissed or overturned on appeal.

paragraph.text: The court shall order a hearing on such motion, and may take evidence regarding the allegations included therein.

subsection.text: No fee may be assessed or collected from a victim in connection with the issuance of an order under subsection (a).

clause.text: a crime of violence (as such term is defined in section 16(a)) that is a felony under Federal law; or
an offense that includes as an element a sexual act or sexual conduct (as defined in section 2246) that is a felony under Federal law; and

paragraph.text: The term contact means any direct or indirect communication, transmission, or physical interaction between two or more persons, including communication or interaction by written, oral, electronic, digital, or physical means, whether initiated personally, through an intermediary, or by automated or technological systems.

subsection.text: Nothing in this section may be construed to limit the application of an order under this section within the jurisdiction of any State.
The table of sections for such chapter is amended by adding at the end the following:

endorsement.action-desc: Reported with an amendment, committed to the Committee of the Whole House on the State of the Union, and ordered to be printed
